title: melampyrum pratense var. americanum
intro: Melampyrum pratense var. americanum, also known as American cow-wheat, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Orobanchaceae, characterized by its small, greenish-yellow flowers and woody stems. Traditionally, American cow-wheat has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of American cow-wheat include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestive issues, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, American cow-wheat can be used as a tea, tincture, or infused oil to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
